zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Flambert於大約1266年出生在英國艾可時，她的父親James de Flambert I27歲，母親Catherine de Bernard27歲。她於1328年與Sir Hugh de Tyrell在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1330年在英國艾可英格拉福享年65歲。

nameMeanings.name: Flambert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Flambard
meanings: 1 Norman, English: nickname from Old French flambard ‘flaming torch’. There is no evidence to support an origin in a Continental Germanic personal name.
2  French: nickname from the same origin as (1).

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Flambert was born about 1266, in Essex, England, her father, James de Flambert I, was 27 and her mother, Catherine de Bernard, was 27. She married Sir Hugh de Tyrell in 1328, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died in 1330, in Ingrave, Essex, England, at the age of 65.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Flambert est née environ 1266, à Essex, Angleterre, son père, James de Flambert I, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Catherine de Bernard, avait 27 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Hugh de Tyrell en 1328, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle est décédée en 1330, à Ingrave, Essex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 65 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Flambert(이)가 대략 1266년 영국 에세스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 James de Flambert I(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Catherine de Bernard(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 1328년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Sir Hugh de Tyrell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1330년 영국 에세스 잉그레이브에서 65세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Flambert родилась около 1266 г. в местности эссекс, Англия, её отцу по имени James de Flambert I было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Catherine de Bernard, 27. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Hugh de Tyrell в 1328 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла в 1330 г. в местности инграв, эссекс, Англия в возрасте 65 лет.
